name: Ojos del Cabriel, Herrería and San Pedro Waterfalls Loop
name_original: Ojos de Cabriel - Cascada de la Herrería y del Molino de San Pedro — circular desde El Vallecillo
description: This moderate 16.3-mile mountain bike route from El Vallecillo explores the Ojos de Cabriel, Herrería, and San Pedro waterfalls.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around El Vallecillo offer diverse terrain within a protected natural area, characterized by elevations ranging from 1400 to 1600 meters. The landscape features extensive coniferous forests of Scots pine and black pine, alongside karstic reliefs including dolines and sinkholes. The Cabriel River originates here, carving impressive valleys and creating notable waterfalls, providing varied natural surfaces for mountain biking. This region transitions towards the Serranía de Cuenca, contributing to its rugged and picturesque environment. Best mountain bike trails around El Vallecillo The most popular mountain bike trail is Ojos del Cabriel, Herrería and San Pedro Waterfalls Loop, a 16.3 miles (26.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 34 minutes to complete. This route explores the natural springs of Ojos del Cabriel and two significant waterfalls. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Montes Universales — vuelta desde el Camping Algarbe, a moderate 19.3 miles (31.0 km) path. This route winds through the scenic Montes Universales region, offering varied forest and open terrain. Local mountain bikers also love the Stone bridge – Spring and washhouse loop from El Vallecillo, a 35.2 miles (56.7 km) trail leading through varied landscapes including a stone bridge and natural springs, often completed in about 5 hours 21 minutes. Mountain biking around El Vallecillo is defined by coniferous forests, the Cabriel River, and karstic reliefs. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging routes. The routes in El Vallecillo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from a few reviews. More than dozens of mountain bikers have used komoot to explore El Vallecillo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around El Vallecillo?
A: There are 14 mountain bike trails available around El Vallecillo, offering diverse experiences for riders. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in El Vallecillo?
A: The terrain around El Vallecillo is quite varied, ranging from 1400 to 1600 meters in elevation. You'll ride through extensive coniferous forests, encounter karstic reliefs like dolines and sinkholes, and follow paths alongside the Cabriel River, which carves impressive valleys and creates waterfalls. This mix provides a rugged and picturesque environment with natural surfaces.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in El Vallecillo?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from 5 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, the scenic beauty of the coniferous forests, and the natural features like the Cabriel River and its waterfalls.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in El Vallecillo?
A: While there are no trails specifically graded as 'easy' in our current selection, there are 10 'moderate' routes that could be suitable for riders with some experience. These routes offer a good balance of challenge and accessibility, allowing you to enjoy the region's beauty without extreme difficulty.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders in El Vallecillo?
A: Yes, for experienced riders seeking a challenge, there are 4 'difficult' mountain bike routes around El Vallecillo. One such route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40304394" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Stone bridge – Spring and washhouse loop from El Vallecillo&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 35.2 miles (56.7 km) with significant elevation changes.

Q: Are there any notable natural attractions or landmarks along the mountain bike trails?
A: Absolutely. Many routes feature significant natural attractions. You can explore the source of the Cabriel River at &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4547251" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojos del Cabriel&lt;/a&gt;, or visit the spectacular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/604533" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Pedro Mill Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5247809" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Herrería Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. The region is also part of the LIC-ZEPA Valdecabriel-Las Tejedas protected zone, known for its unspoiled beauty.

Q: Can I find mountain bike routes that feature waterfalls?
A: Yes, several routes incorporate the region's beautiful waterfalls. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1003543651/ojos-del-cabriel-herreria-and-san-pedro-waterfalls-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojos del Cabriel, Herrería and San Pedro Waterfalls Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a 16.3-mile trail specifically designed to visit the Herrería and San Pedro Waterfalls, as well as the Ojos del Cabriel.

Q: Are the mountain bike trails around El Vallecillo suitable for families?
A: The trails around El Vallecillo are generally rated as moderate to difficult. While there are no easy-rated trails, families with older children who are experienced mountain bikers might find some of the moderate routes enjoyable. Always check the specific route details for distance and elevation before heading out.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails in El Vallecillo?
A: El Vallecillo is situated within a protected natural area (LIC-ZEPA Valdecabriel-Las Tejedas). While many outdoor areas in Spain allow dogs on trails, it's always best to keep them on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure their safety, especially in areas with varied terrain and potential livestock. Check local signage for any specific restrictions.

Q: Are there circular mountain bike routes available in El Vallecillo?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes around El Vallecillo are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1003543651/ojos-del-cabriel-herreria-and-san-pedro-waterfalls-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojos del Cabriel, Herrería and San Pedro Waterfalls Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the longer &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40304394" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Stone bridge – Spring and washhouse loop from El Vallecillo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is there parking available near the mountain bike trails in El Vallecillo?
A: El Vallecillo is a small village, and parking is generally available within or on the outskirts of the village. Many routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1003543651/ojos-del-cabriel-herreria-and-san-pedro-waterfalls-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojos del Cabriel, Herrería and San Pedro Waterfalls Loop&lt;/a&gt;, start directly from the village, making access convenient for those arriving by car.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in El Vallecillo?
A: Given its elevation (1400 to 1600 meters), El Vallecillo experiences distinct seasons. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for mountain biking. Summers can be warm, but the elevation provides some relief. Winters can bring cold temperatures and potentially snow, making some trails less accessible.

Q: Are there any routes that explore the Montes Universales region?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1008304509/montes-universales-vuelta-desde-el-camping-algarbe" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montes Universales — vuelta desde el Camping Algarbe&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 19.3-mile (31.0 km) route that winds through the scenic Montes Universales, offering varied forest and open terrain for mountain bikers.
tips: Viewpoint with views of the Cabriel River valley in the area of its Ojos (or springs). From here you can go down a very cool path to the Ojos and the Cascada de la Herrería without technical difficulty.

The Cascada de la Herrería is a natural gem located in the Sierra de Albarracín, near the town of El Vallecillo. This beautiful waterfall, also known as the “Hidden Waterfall,” has a drop of approximately 8 meters over the Cabriel River. 
To reach the waterfall, you can follow a hiking route that begins in El Vallecillo. The route is short, just half a kilometer, and takes you through a varied and picturesque landscape. During the walk, you will also be able to see the natural springs known as the Ojos del Cabriel, which feed the river and the waterfall.

Without a doubt one of the most beautiful waterfalls with a pool I have been to in Spain. Its location, in what is called "emptied Spain" due to its low population density, has prevented its overcrowding, although in summer there were quite a few people bathing. It is advisable to wear swimming or canyoning shoes to enter and exit the water through the porous stones. The view from the cavity behind the waterfall is simply stunning.

Church of the town of El Vallecillo, a very quiet place with few inhabitants, one more example of the uninhabited area. From here several trails lead to the Cascada del Molino de San Pedro and the Ojos del Cabriel

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: This circular mountain biking route, covering approximately 26.2 kilometers, is estimated to take around 2 hours and 34 minutes to complete. This duration is for the biking activity itself and does not include stops for sightseeing.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this mountain biking trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is rated as moderate. It requires good fitness and advanced riding skills. While it features well-maintained dirt tracks and engaging sections, some portions might require pushing your bike, especially if you're not accustomed to longer, more challenging variants.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail starts and ends in El Vallecillo, so you can typically find parking within the village. It's advisable to check local signage for designated parking areas suitable for trail access.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: The route primarily consists of well-maintained dirt tracks, offering a generally rideable experience. However, be prepared for some spectacular and enjoyable shorter sections of singletrack trails. You'll also traverse varied landscapes, including a descent through a coniferous forest with black pines.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: This route showcases several natural and historical highlights. You'll encounter the Ojos del Cabriel Viewpoint and the Ojos del Cabriel themselves, which are significant natural springs. The trail also passes by the beautiful Herrería Waterfall and the impressive San Pedro Mill Waterfall. In El Vallecillo, you can also see the Church of the Holy Trinity.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is particularly vibrant in autumn when the lush surroundings of the Cabriel River are at their most colorful. However, the Sierra de Albarracín offers beautiful scenery throughout the warmer months, making spring and early summer also good choices, weather permitting.

faqs.question: Is this mountain biking trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for dogs on this exact trail are not detailed, generally, dogs are allowed on trails in the Sierra de Albarracín. It's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially in natural areas, to protect wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all trail users.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail or the Sierra de Albarracín?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are generally required to access this mountain biking trail or the broader Sierra de Albarracín natural area. However, always respect local regulations and private property.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of other established paths. You will ride along parts of the Rubiales - Orihuela del Tremedal, Camino del molino de La Herrería, and Camino de los Ojos del Cabriel trails.

faqs.question: What makes the scenery along this route special?
faqs.answer: The scenery is a major highlight, traversing the beautiful landscapes of the Sierra de Albarracín. You'll ride through coniferous forests, notably featuring striking black pines, and follow the Cabriel River, renowned as one of Europe's cleanest, contributing to the pristine environment.

faqs.question: What gear should I bring for mountain biking this route?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and varied terrain, it's recommended to bring a well-maintained mountain bike, a helmet, appropriate cycling attire, water, snacks, a basic repair kit, and navigation tools. Sun protection and layers of clothing are also advisable.

waypoints.name: Church of the Holy Trinity (El Vallecillo)
